summary: The reproduction bundle builds ZooKeeper from the pinned vulnerable upstream commit, starts a standalone server with default admin settings, and verifies unauthenticated access to admin commands.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should expose unauthenticated admin HTTP endpoints on the default admin port, return JVM system properties, and accept an attacker-chosen traceMask update through the admin API.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built ZooKeeper from the pinned upstream commit, started a standalone server, reached http://127.0.0.1:8080/commands/get_trace_mask without authentication, returned JSON from /commands/system_properties containing java.version and user.dir, and changed the trace mask to 31337 through /commands/set_trace_mask.
verification_method: The verification helper waits for the admin endpoint, fetches /commands/system_properties to confirm unauthenticated metadata disclosure, then calls /commands/set_trace_mask and /commands/get_trace_mask to confirm unauthenticated state mutation through the admin API.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed a JSON proof object containing sample_system_properties with java.version=17.0.19 and user.dir=/tmp/tmp.hjbUJUQ0fc/repo.
The run printed set_trace_mask_response and get_trace_mask_response objects that both reported tracemask=31337.
The run printed the success signal: EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS unauthenticated admin HTTP access exposed system properties and accepted traceMask mutation 31337.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
